questions.question: Which of the following best describes the role of an operating system in a computer system?
questions.option_a: A. It directly replaces computer hardware
questions.option_b: B. It acts as an interface between users and computer hardware
questions.option_c: C. It only runs application programs
questions.option_d: D. It is used only to manage files
questions.explanation: An operating system acts as an intermediary between users and the computer hardware, managing resources and providing services to applications.

questions.question: Which component of a computer system provides the basic computing resources such as CPU and memory?
questions.explanation: Hardware provides fundamental computing resources such as CPU, memory, and I/O devices.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four components of a computer system?
questions.explanation: The four main components are hardware, operating system, application programs, and users.

questions.question: Which goal of an operating system focuses on making the computer system easy for users to work with?
questions.explanation: One of the OS goals is to make the computer system convenient and easy for users.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an application program?
questions.explanation: Web browsers are application programs that allow users to perform tasks using system resources.

questions.question: Which of the following is a purpose of using an operating system?
questions.explanation: Operating systems manage resources such as CPU, memory, and I/O devices.

questions.question: Which type of operating system allows users to freely download and modify its source code?
questions.explanation: Open-source operating systems provide access to source code for modification and distribution.

questions.question: Which type of operating system requires purchasing a license because its source code is restricted?
questions.explanation: Closed-source operating systems have restricted source code and usually require purchase.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a user interface in an operating system?
questions.option_b: B. Allowing users to interact with hardware and software
questions.explanation: The user interface enables users to interact with the computer system.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a Command Line Interface (CLI)?
questions.option_c: C. Requires more resources than GUI
questions.option_d: D. Operates only with a mouse
questions.explanation: CLI requires users to remember and type commands.

questions.question: Which component is mainly responsible for managing processes in an operating system?
questions.explanation: Process management handles process creation, scheduling, and termination.

questions.question: Which OS component manages the allocation and deallocation of main memory?
questions.explanation: Main-memory management is responsible for memory allocation and protection.

questions.question: Which service allows an operating system to detect hardware or program errors and take corrective action?
questions.explanation: Error detection services identify hardware and software faults and respond appropriately.

questions.question: Which OS service enables programs to read, write, create, and delete files?
questions.explanation: File-system manipulation provides capabilities for file and directory operations.

questions.question: What is a system call?
questions.option_a: A. A hardware instruction executed by the CPU
questions.option_b: B. A user interface to operating system services
questions.option_c: C. A type of hardware interrupt
questions.explanation: System calls provide a mechanism for user programs to request services from the operating system.

questions.question: Which of the following describes normal termination of a system call?
questions.option_b: B. Program exits or returns successfully
questions.explanation: Normal termination occurs when a program completes execution and exits properly.

questions.question: Which of the following utilities is used to create and modify files?
questions.explanation: Text editors allow users to create and edit files.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a system program used for status information?
questions.explanation: System programs provide information such as date, time, memory usage, and disk space.

questions.question: In dual-mode operation, which mode is used by the operating system core?
questions.explanation: Monitor mode (also called supervisor or privileged mode) is used by the operating system.

questions.question: Which hardware mechanism indicates whether the CPU is operating in user mode or monitor mode?
questions.explanation: The mode bit identifies the current execution mode of the CPU.

questions.question: Which instructions can only be executed in monitor mode?
questions.explanation: Privileged instructions are restricted to monitor mode to protect system resources.

questions.question: What happens when an interrupt or fault occurs in the system?
questions.option_b: B. Hardware switches to monitor mode
questions.explanation: When interrupts occur, hardware switches the CPU to monitor mode to allow OS handling.

questions.question: Why are user programs required to use system calls for I/O operations?
questions.option_b: B. Because direct I/O operations are restricted
questions.explanation: Direct I/O operations are privileged instructions, so user programs must request them via system calls.

questions.question: Which registers are used to enforce memory protection in an operating system?
questions.option_a: A. Stack and program counter registers
questions.explanation: Base and limit registers define the legal memory address range for a program.

questions.question: What is the role of the base register in memory protection?
questions.option_a: A. Stores the highest memory address
questions.option_b: B. Holds the smallest legal physical memory address
questions.explanation: The base register contains the lowest valid memory address accessible to the program.

questions.question: What mechanism prevents a single user from monopolizing the CPU?
questions.explanation: A timer interrupt ensures the OS regains control periodically to manage CPU usage.

questions.question: When the timer reaches zero, what event occurs?
questions.explanation: When the timer expires, an interrupt is triggered to transfer control to the OS.

questions.question: Which type of interrupt is generated by external hardware devices?
questions.explanation: Hardware interrupts are generated by external devices such as I/O controllers.

questions.question: Which interrupt type occurs when a program requests an OS service?
questions.explanation: Software interrupts occur when programs request OS services via system calls.

questions.question: What is a trap in operating systems?
questions.option_a: A. An interrupt generated by hardware devices
questions.option_b: B. An interrupt generated by the CPU due to errors or conditions
questions.explanation: Traps are CPU-generated interrupts that signal errors or exceptional conditions.

questions.question: What happens to the running program when an interrupt occurs?
questions.option_d: D. It is transferred to disk
questions.explanation: Interrupts suspend the current program so the OS can handle the event.

questions.question: Where are the addresses of interrupt service routines stored?
questions.explanation: The interrupt vector contains addresses of interrupt service routines.

questions.question: What does ISR stand for in operating systems?
questions.explanation: ISR stands for Interrupt Service Routine, which handles interrupts.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of resource allocation in an operating system?
questions.option_b: B. Assigning system resources to multiple users or jobs
questions.explanation: Resource allocation ensures fair and efficient distribution of resources.

questions.question: Which OS service keeps track of how much computing resources each user consumes?
questions.explanation: Accounting tracks resource usage for billing or statistics.

questions.question: Which OS service ensures that access to system resources is controlled?
questions.explanation: Protection and security mechanisms control access permissions.

questions.question: Which design goal focuses on making an operating system easy to use and reliable for users?
questions.explanation: User goals emphasize usability, safety, and reliability for users.

questions.question: Which OS design goal focuses on making the system easy to implement and maintain?
questions.explanation: System goals focus on implementation efficiency, flexibility, and reliability.

questions.question: In operating system design, what does a mechanism define?
questions.option_a: A. What tasks should be performed
questions.option_d: D. Which hardware devices are used
questions.explanation: Mechanisms define how tasks are performed in the system.

questions.question: What does policy determine in operating system design?
questions.option_b: B. What actions should be taken
questions.explanation: Policy determines what decisions should be made in the system.
